data.annual_summary: Tokyo experiences four distinct seasons, each offering a unique charm for travelers. Winters (December-February) are cool and dry, with occasional light snow. Spring (March-May) is mild and pleasant, highlighted by the iconic cherry blossom season in late March to early April. Summers (June-August) are hot, very humid, and marked by a distinct rainy season (tsuyu) in June, followed by the potential for typhoons later in the season. Autumn (September-November) brings relief from the heat, offering crisp, clear days, beautiful fall foliage, and generally stable weather after the early September typhoon risks. The best times to visit are generally spring and autumn, providing comfortable temperatures and lower humidity.

January.season_notes: Coldest month, but typically dry and clear; good for experiencing traditional Japanese New Year.

February.season_notes: Still cold, with a slight chance of snow; excellent for indoor activities and winter festivals.

March.season_notes: Warming temperatures and increasing anticipation for cherry blossoms towards month-end.

April.season_notes: Peak cherry blossom season in early April, followed by pleasant, mild weather and Golden Week holidays.

May.season_notes: Warm and increasingly humid, but generally very pleasant before the onset of the rainy season.

June.season_notes: The rainy season (tsuyu) brings high humidity and frequent, often heavy, rainfall.

July.season_notes: Hot and very humid after the rainy season ends, with intense summer heat.

August.season_notes: The hottest and most humid month, with high chances of thunderstorms and potential typhoon activity.

September.season_notes: Still warm and humid with the highest rainfall of the year and continued typhoon risk, especially early in the month.

October.season_notes: Pleasant, clear, and crisp autumn weather emerges, with comfortable temperatures and decreasing humidity.

November.season_notes: Cooler temperatures bring beautiful autumn foliage, making it a popular time for outdoor sightseeing.

December.season_notes: Cool and relatively dry, with festive illuminations making the city magical despite the chill.

travel_implications: **For your May 2026 trip:** Pack layers, including light long sleeves for sun protection (UV Index is Very High) and cooler evenings, along with comfortable walking shoes, as temperatures can range from warm to mild.
Be prepared for potential rain showers, as May sees an average of 10 rainy days. An umbrella or light rain jacket is a must-have, especially towards the latter half of the month.
While May is beautiful, it's also considered 'peak season' due to pleasant weather and numerous festivals. Expect higher prices for accommodation and flights, and book well in advance.

Avoid the Golden Week national holiday period if your May 2026 trip falls within it (typically late April to early May), as popular attractions will be extremely crowded and transportation heavily booked.
Hydrate frequently, even if it doesn't feel scorchingly hot, as increasing humidity (70%) and a Very High UV Index can lead to dehydration and sunburn if precautions aren't taken.
